channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Italian Premier Meloni visits Pope Francis in hospital and says he's in good spirits, responsive
item.description: ROME (AP) — Italian Premier Giorgia Meloni visited Pope Francis in the hospital Wednesday and reported he was “alert and responsive” and full of good humor, despite his diagnosis of pneumonia and a complicated bronchial infection that has sidelined the 88-year-old pontiff for six days.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, ANDREA ROSA and PAOLO SANTALUCIA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A worried NATO holds large-scale combat drills as the US stance on Europe shifts under Trump
item.description: SMARDAN, Romania (AP) — NATO members continued their largest combat exercises of 2025 on Wednesday, testing their ability to rapidly deploy large-scale forces on the 32-nation alliance’s eastern border as worries grow over its most powerful member, the United States.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH and NICOLAE DUMITRACHE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Hawley calls for new inspector general to investigate Ukraine spending
item.description: Sen. Josh Hawley (R-Mo.) pledged to introduce legislation to track U.S. dollars designated for Ukraine in its war against Russia, after citing the possibility of corrupt spending under the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: A worried NATO holds large-scale combat drills as the US stance on Europe shifts under Trump
item.description: NATO members are continuing their largest combat exercises of 2025 amid concern within the 32-nation alliance over its most powerful member, the United States.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Mcgrath And Nicolae Dumitrache, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pakistan steps up arrests of Afghans without papers. Kabul says it's a move to expel all refugees
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Authorities have stepped up arrests of Afghan citizens in Pakistan's capital and a nearby city in an effort that the Afghan Embassy in Islamabad described on Wednesday as a push to force the expulsion of all Afghan refugees from the country.
item.dc:creator: MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: EU envoys approve more sanctions against Russia to mark the third anniversary of its war on Ukraine
item.description: European Union envoys have approved new sanctions against Russia, with the measures set to enter into force next week on the third anniversary of its full-scale invasion of Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Lorne Cook, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Philippine village battles dengue by offering bounties for mosquitos
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — A village in the densely populated Philippine capital region launched a battle against dengue Wednesday by offering a token bounty to residents for captured mosquitos — dead or alive.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Man who attacked Japan's ex-Prime Minister Kishida is convicted and sentenced to 10 years in prison
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — A Japanese court on Wednesday convicted a man who threw a homemade pipe bomb at Japan's former Prime Minister Fumio Kishida at a 2023 campaign event, sentencing him to 10 years in prison, court officials said.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Police in disputed Kashmir raid bookstores, seizing books related to Islamic group
item.description: Police in Indian-controlled Kashmir have raided bookstores and seized 668 books linked to a major Islamic organization in the disputed region, where strict controls on the press have escalated in recent years.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A deeper look at the talks between US and Russian officials as Trump suggests Ukraine is to blame
item.description: RIYADH, Saudi Arabia (AP) — Top U.S. and Russian officials had their most extensive high-level engagement since Moscow sent troops into Ukraine almost three years ago, meeting for four hours Tuesday before President Donald Trump suggested that Kyiv was to blame for the conflict.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and VLADIMIR ISACHENKOV, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Hamas says it will free 6 living hostages and hand over 4 bodies, accelerating Gaza releases
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — A top Hamas official says the militant group will free six living Israeli hostages on Saturday and return the bodies of four others on Thursday, a surprise acceleration in releases apparently in trade for Israel’s allowing mobile homes and construction equipment into the devastated Gaza
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Germany's economic minister pushes for European X, end of Silicon Valley dominance
item.description: Robert Habeck, Germany’s economic minister and vice chancellor, pushed for Europe to create its own version of the social media platform X and urged the continent to break away from Silicon Valley’s dominance.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Chinese navy helicopter flies within 10 feet of Philippine patrol plane over disputed shoal
item.description: A Chinese navy helicopter flew within 10 feet of a Philippine patrol plane over the disputed Scarborough Shoal in the South China Sea; the plane did not have to alter its path to avoid a collision.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rubio makes progress with Russia after Trump promised to bring end to war in Ukraine
item.description: Secretary of State Macro Rubio said first step in ending the war in Ukraine is reestablishing diplomacy with Russia by reopening embassies in Moscow and Washington, as EU watches with concern.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US Hockey team wants Trump at Canada final after fights
item.description: Bill Guerin, general manager of the U.S. men's national ice hockey team, invited President Trump to Thursday's 4 Nations Face-Off championship game in Boston following the president's history-making visit to New Orleans for the Super Bowl. “We would love it if President Trump was in attendance.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Mexico awaits new response from Google on dispute over Gulf of Mexico name before filing lawsuit
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexico said Monday it's awaiting a new response from Google to its request that the tech company fully restore the name Gulf of Mexico to its Google Maps service before filing a lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Attacks by Sudanese RSF paramilitaries leave hundreds dead in White Nile State
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Attacks by Sudan's paramilitary Rapid Support Force have killed hundreds of civilians, including infants, in White Nile state, Sudanese officials and rights groups said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: French lawmakers mull banning headscarves in sport. Amnesty International says it's discriminatory
item.description: Amnesty International is urging French lawmakers to reject a bill this week that would ban headscarves in all sporting competitions. The bill is backed by right-wing senators and will be debated from Tuesday in the upper house of the French parliament.
item.dc:creator: SAMUEL PETREQUIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Toronto plane crash: Harrowing video shows Delta plane erupting into fireball, flipping upside down
item.description: All 80 people aboard the Delta plane that flipped upside down and crash landed in Toronto survived, but three, including a child, are critically injured.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chinese navy helicopter flies within 10 feet of Philippine patrol plane over disputed shoal
item.description: OVER THE SCARBOROUGH SHOAL, South China Sea (AP) — A Chinese navy helicopter flew within 10 feet (3 meters) of a Philippine patrol plane on Tuesday in a disputed area of the South China Sea, prompting the Filipino pilot to warn by radio: “You are flying too close, you are very dangerous.
item.dc:creator: JOEAL CALUPITAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korea slams US and Asian rivals for pursuing 'absurd' plans to denuclearize North
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea on Tuesday criticized the United States and Asian neighbors for pursuing the “absurd” goal of denuclearizing the North and said it will push to expand its nuclear forces under the direction of its authoritarian leader Kim Jong Un.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tourist loses both hands while snorkeling in Turks and Caicos after shark attacks during failed photo op
item.description: A woman from Canada lost both her hands when she was attacked by a shark after trying to take a picture with the creature while swimming in Turks and Caicos.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Delta plane crash: What we know about CRJ-900, aircraft that flipped over in Toronto
item.description: The CRJ-900 can seat either 70 or 76 people, but the layout and basic dimensions for the craft are nearly identical no matter the capacity.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Pope remains in hospital to treat 'complex clinical' condition: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis’ respiratory tract infection is presenting a “complex clinical picture” that will require further hospitalization, the Vatican said Monday as concerns grew about the increasingly frail health of the 88-year-old pontiff.
item.dc:creator: By NICOLE WINFIELD Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US leaders in Saudi Arabia for peace talks on Russia-Ukraine war
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said he will not accept a deal without a seat at the negotiating table.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli military experts weigh in on Trump's ‘all hell’ threat to Hamas and what it could look like
item.description: Former Israeli military officials discuss how President Donald Trump's threats against Hamas could play out in Gaza, and what strategies the IDF could adopt if they go back into the enclave.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rwanda-backed M23 rebels breach second major city in Congo's mineral-rich east
item.description: M23 rebels have reportedly "occupied" Bukavu, Democratic Republic of the Congo, positioning themselves at the governor's office and vowing to deviate from the "old regime.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Food delivery driver helped stop suspect following deadly stabbing: officials
item.description: A 23-year-old with possible connections to the Islamic State group is in custody after a stabbing of six passersby that left a 14-year-old boy dead.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Doctors order 'absolute rest' for Pope Francis while he's being treated for a respiratory infection
item.description: ROME (AP) — Doctors on Saturday prescribed “absolute rest” for Pope Francis and modified the treatment for his respiratory tract infection, the Vatican said, a day after the 88-year-old pope was admitted to a hospital following a weeklong bout of bronchitis.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Zelenskyy to Vance: Ukraine wants 'security guarantees' as Trump seeks to end Ukraine-Russia war
item.description: MUNICH (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Friday that his country wants “security guarantees” before any talks with Russia, as the Trump administration presses both countries to find a quick endgame to the three-year war.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, EMMA BURROWS and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A drone pierced the outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant. Radiation levels are normal
item.description: CHERNOBYL NUCLEAR POWER STATION, Ukraine (AP) — A drone armed with a warhead hit the protective outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant early Friday, punching a hole in the structure and briefly starting a fire, in an attack Kyiv blamed on Russia. The Kremlin denied it was responsible.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and EFREM LUKATSKY, Associated Press, WGN 9
